*May 6, 2017*

Django 1.11.1 adds a minor feature and fixes several bugs in 1.11.

Allowed disabling server-side cursors on PostgreSQL
===================================================

The change in Django 1.11 to make :meth:`.QuerySet.iterator()` use server-side
cursors on PostgreSQL prevents running Django with `pgBouncer` in transaction
pooling mode. To reallow that, use the :setting:`DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS
<DATABASE-DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS>` setting in :setting:`DATABASES`.

See :ref:`transaction-pooling-server-side-cursors` for more discussion.

Bugfixes
========

* Made migrations respect ``Index``’s ``name`` argument. If you created a
  named index with Django 1.11, ``makemigrations`` will create a migration to
  recreate the index with the correct name (:ticket:`28051`).

* Fixed a crash when using a ``__icontains`` lookup on a ``ArrayField``
  (:ticket:`28038`).

* Fixed a crash when using a two-tuple in ``EmailMessage``’s ``attachments``
  argument (:ticket:`28042`).

* Fixed ``QuerySet.filter()`` crash when it references the name of a
  ``OneToOneField`` primary key (:ticket:`28047`).

* Fixed empty POST data table appearing instead of "No POST data" in HTML debug
  page (:ticket:`28079`).

* Restored ``BoundField``\s without any ``choices`` evaluating to ``True``
  (:ticket:`28058`).

* Prevented ``SessionBase.cycle_key()`` from losing session data if
  ``_session_cache`` isn't populated (:ticket:`28066`).

* Fixed layout of ``ReadOnlyPasswordHashWidget`` (used in the admin's user
  change page) (:ticket:`28097`).

* Allowed prefetch calls on managers with custom ``ModelIterable`` subclasses
  (:ticket:`28096`).

* Fixed change password link in the ``contrib.auth`` admin for ``el``,
  ``es_MX``, and ``pt`` translations (:ticket:`28100`).

* Restored the output of the ``class`` attribute in the ``<ul>`` of widgets
  that use the ``multiple_input.html`` template. This fixes
  ``ModelAdmin.radio_fields`` with ``admin.HORIZONTAL`` (:ticket:`28059`).

* Fixed crash in ``BaseGeometryWidget.subwidgets()`` (:ticket:`28039`).

* Fixed exception reraising in ORM query execution when ``cursor.execute()``
  fails and the subsequent ``cursor.close()`` also fails (:ticket:`28091`).

* Fixed a regression where ``CheckboxSelectMultiple``, ``NullBooleanSelect``,
  ``RadioSelect``, ``SelectMultiple``, and ``Select`` localized option values
  (:ticket:`28075`).

* Corrected the stack level of unordered queryset pagination warnings
  (:ticket:`28109`).

* Fixed a regression causing incorrect queries for ``__in`` subquery lookups
  when models use ``ForeignKey.to_field`` (:ticket:`28101`).

* Fixed crash when overriding the template of
  ``django.views.static.directory_index()`` (:ticket:`28122`).

* Fixed a regression in formset ``min_num`` validation with unchanged forms
  that have initial data (:ticket:`28130`).

* Prepared for ``cx_Oracle`` 6.0 support (:ticket:`28138`).

* Updated the ``contrib.postgres`` ``SplitArrayWidget`` to use template-based
  widget rendering (:ticket:`28040`).

* Fixed crash in ``BaseGeometryWidget.get_context()`` when overriding existing
  ``attrs`` (:ticket:`28105`).

* Prevented ``AddIndex`` and ``RemoveIndex`` from mutating model state
  (:ticket:`28043`).

* Prevented migrations from dropping database indexes from ``Meta.indexes``
  when changing ``Field.db_index`` to ``False`` (:ticket:`28052`).

* Fixed a regression in choice ordering in form fields with grouped and
  non-grouped options (:ticket:`28157`).

* Fixed crash in  ``BaseInlineFormSet._construct_form()`` when using
  ``save_as_new`` (:ticket:`28159`).

* Fixed a regression where ``Model._state.db`` wasn't set correctly on
  multi-table inheritance parent models after saving a child model
  (:ticket:`28166`).

* Corrected the return type of ``ArrayField(CITextField())`` values retrieved
  from the database (:ticket:`28161`).

* Fixed ``QuerySet.prefetch_related()`` crash when fetching relations in nested
  ``Prefetch`` objects (:ticket:`27554`).

* Prevented hiding GDAL errors if it's not installed when using ``contrib.gis``
  (:ticket:`28160`). (It's a required dependency as of Django 1.11.)

* Fixed a regression causing ``__in`` lookups on a foreign key to fail when
  using the foreign key's parent model as the lookup value (:ticket:`28175`).

release to support Python 3.6. We **highly recommend** and only officially

pr_body: This is something I need for my own tools. It will send a "timesync" message and print the response to commandline. My tools use this to correlate the drone time with the local time. I have a fork with this change, but proposing to add it if others can use it.

pr_title: Only add replica load balancer if a corresponding variable is set to 'True' or 'true'.
Move context to top level datalab.Context, remove Project and Projects
pr_body:
This is a long PR but it's mostly renaming, I'll detail the changes here to make it easier to review:

- Removed no longer needed classes: `Project` and `Projects`. The only functionality we needed there was getting the project id, and that can be done with using the projects API.
- Removed the `%projects` magic
- Moved set of used utilities in _context into `datalab.utils._utils`
- `google.datalab._context.Context` -> `google.datalab.Context` (this is what's making the PR so long)
- Added a dictionary for global config `google.datalab.Context.default().config`

pr_title: Rename lockfile to package throughout repo
pr_body:
### What was wrong?

pr_title: Refactor out delays between task executions
pr_body:
After some delays with beetmover executions, there's a way we can save some wall time with sequential scriptworker tasks.

Looking at task graph FvLr0IZYQiWC-OfjF-NT5Q there were between 238 and 252 tasks for each beetmoverworker.  Picking an individual beetmoverworker - number 3, the first task was at 11:40:42 and the final one started at 14:22:25, a total wall time of 2h41m43s. 

The tasks themselves are small, taking a mean of 23s. Looking further there was a mean delay between one task ending and the next beginning, of 12.28s. Not meaningful when there are a few big tasks, but with ~250 per node, it adds up to a wall time of roughly 51 minutes.

The main loop in the scriptworker is doing this:
 
if I have a task:
   do the task
   sleep(1)
sleep(5)
return

in the outer function, sleep(5) again, meaning that between a successful task ending and the next work being claimed, the scriptworker is sleeping for 11 seconds.

This PR adjusts that, so that the poll_interval, which controls the sleep duration, has doubled, but there's only one sleep call. The sleep also only gets called if there was no work to do. The new workflow looks like this:

try to claim a task
if no work, sleep for 10s and return
if work, do work, sleeping for 1s between tasks of there's more than one.

This means that if there's a task to do there's only a 1s sleep between it finishing and trying to get the next task - we avoid hammering the taskcluster api because the tasks themselves take longer than the poll interval. If there's no work to do, we wait a polite interval before trying again.

An optimistic estimate is that during busy times, such as all the beetmover jobs pending, we should ve able to save ~40 minutes of wall time.

pr_title: Add small timeout on web3 test request
pr_body: This should fix eternally hanging web3 test requests when web3 RPC endpoint passed by Raiden isn't acessible nor answering (DROP) from the client's perspective. On timeout, it'll fallback to `config.web3_fallback`, which defaults to `http://localhost:8545`, and make **testnet** webUI working again.
Fixes #2111

pr_title: Do not invoke contract compilation at file import
pr_body:
Until now anything that imported `raiden/blockchain/abi.py` invoked the
compilation of our contracts in order to get the ABI and some other
attributes.

This PR moves the logic to a class that is initialized at import but not
instantiated there. The ABI and other contract attributes are lazily
evaluated. In other words the first time something is required only then
are the contracts compiled.

Finally now we can do `raiden --help` without waiting 5-6 seconds.

pr_title: Added info about datetime limits to make output.
Round out the breaking changes for ResourceV2
pr_body:
This PR pains me to write, since it is a combination of two commits that arguably don't belong in the same PR, so I apologize in advance for that. (I do think that they are best reviewed together, though, so I'm keeping them together here.)

There are two commits in this PR. The first, https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/commit/9ca76b65c0a1a498cec4a9dca9b594455d55f15e, contains the remainder of the breaking changes we're going to absorb for the V2 version of `Resource`. The second, https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/commit/48d095d66fed5cac8f2a94a14ee954f8db65aae8, is a partial revert of https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/pull/1647 - it removes the `Down` migrate operation since it is not generally sound.

The original idea behind the `Down` operation (as written in https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/pull/1647) was that it was a way to perform a semantically valid transformation from a higher-versioned API type to a lower-versioned API type. While generally OK in practice at the time it was written, in general this is not a valid operation. This is already broken with `External`, but with the addition of `Provider` and `Status` the `Down` operation is even more fraught with peril:

1. It's not clear to me what `Down` would do with a resource that has the `Provider` field set,
2. First-class provider resources are not annotated in the snapshot (AFAIK, please correct me if I'm wrong), so a V1 deployment could end up with provider resources in the snapshot.
3. It's legal for a resource to declare a dependency on an `External` resource, so it's possible for a V1 deployment to end up having resources that depend on resources that don't exist.

Since this behavior is dangerous and generally dubious, and because it's made even more dubious with the addition of new fields to `ResourceV2`, I opted to remove all of this code in the second commit. Alongside this removal is a partial revert of the logic in https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/pull/1647, which caused the engine to operate on V2 resources by default and downgrade a V2 deployment to V1 when speaking to the service. This is basically back to status quo prior to #1647.
Bumping to 1.14 make it possible to install from binary in macOS El Capitan which is the last blocking step for the release of the pulumi brew formula.

pr_title: Separate raylet path and non-raylet path in python common extension.
pr_body: Use polymorphism to separate raylet and non-raylet code path in python common extension. It will make the code more readable and maintainable.

To run, download cifar10 with `curl -o cifar-10-binary.tar.gz https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~kriz/cifar-10-binary.tar.gz` in the same directory, unzip it to `cifar10`, and then run: 
`python resnet_main.py --train_data_path=cifar10/data_batch* --eval_data_path=cifar10/test_batch.bin --num_gpus=8` 
with the appropriate number of gpus. It will write results to `results.txt` in the same directory.

pr_title: Fix problem if old version of arrow is cloned
pr_body:
If an old version of arrow is lying around, we get an error like this:

```
+ cd /--/--/--/ray/src/numbuf/thirdparty/arrow
+ git checkout 670612e6fdf699486641ed0d39d22257eb8acdb2
fatal: reference is not a tree: 670612e6fdf699486641ed0d39d22257eb8acdb2
Traceback (most recent call last):
```

In certain situations, the version number of pyarrow can be `None`, see https://github.com/apache/arrow/blob/155bf076204dcb6bf1f68a556e9243556f5e4ebb/python/pyarrow/__init__.py#L29

In this case, constructing the error message didn't work.

pr_title: Use single Timer instance for message deduplication
pr_body:
So far each recevied message received it's own Timer instance for deduplication. It could easily drive into lack of resources on high traffic , as every timer runs on it's own Thread. This commit modifies this mechanism, by using a single timer instance for handling entire deduplication queue.

Signed-off-by: Robert Lubos <robert.lubos@nordicsemi.no>
